findings.title: Anyone can grant themselves unlimited allowance over tokens held by the contract

best_agent_match.title: Anyone can approve themselves to spend all tokens held by the contract

findings.title: Interest is paid from pooled deposits with no enforced reward backing, making the pool structurally insolvent

best_agent_match.title: Interest is paid out of depositor principal, making the staking pool structurally insolvent

findings.title: Deposits continue accruing rewards indefinitely after maturity

best_agent_match.title: Deposits keep earning forever because interest is never capped at lockup expiry

findings.title: Owner can drain all staked USDT at any time

best_agent_match.title: Owner can rug all staked USDT at any time

findings.title: Owner can arbitrarily freeze user principal and rewards via blacklist

best_agent_match.title: Owner can arbitrarily freeze user principal and rewards via blacklist

findings.title: Withdrawing one deposit can permanently brick reward claims for other deposits in the same tier

best_agent_match.title: Owner can blacklist users to permanently lock their funds

rejected_candidates.title: Interest calculation always returns zero - logic error in calculateInterest
rejected_candidates.reason: Not supported. `interestClaimed` is dead code that always evaluates to 0, but `calculateInterest` still returns the separately computed time-based `interest`, so rewards are not forced to zero.

rejected_candidates.title: Syntax error in claimInterestForDeposit prevents compilation
rejected_candidates.reason: Not supported as a reportable issue. The source only shows whitespace in `msg. sender`; that is not sufficient evidence of a real compilation-blocking defect here.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked lockup period validation leads to zero interest rate
rejected_candidates.reason: The code does accept unsupported lockup values between 7 and 90 days and assigns 0 interest, but this is a user-parameter footgun rather than an adversarial protocol-level exploit or insolvency vector.

rejected_candidates.title: No slippage protection in deposit function
rejected_candidates.reason: Not applicable. `deposit` is a direct token transfer into a staking contract, not a price-sensitive swap or exchange path.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Reentrancy Guard on withdraw function
rejected_candidates.reason: Not a realistic issue in this code path. The staked token is hardcoded to mainnet USDT, so the generic malicious-token callback scenario does not apply.

rejected_candidates.title: Referral system implemented but never pays rewards
rejected_candidates.reason: This is incomplete product logic, not a demonstrated security vulnerability causing protocol-level fund loss or denial of service.

rejected_candidates.title: Unused max function indicates leftover/debug code
rejected_candidates.reason: Dead code only; no security impact.

rejected_candidates.title: Hardcoded token address cannot be changed
rejected_candidates.reason: Deployment rigidity alone is not a security finding in this context.

rejected_candidates.title: Inconsistent lockup period storage - single value vs array
rejected_candidates.reason: Redundant bookkeeping is present, but no concrete exploit path or protocol-level harm is shown.

rejected_candidates.title: No event emitted for transferAllFunds
rejected_candidates.reason: Observability issue only; it does not materially change the owner's ability to move funds or create new loss beyond the accepted drain finding.
